Pathfinder 1e — Master Reference

Combat · Skills · Exploration · Reference

Combat Basics
5
▾
Initiative
Dexterity check at the start of battle. Combatants act from highest to lowest result. Ties use highest modifier, then a roll-off.
Surprise Round
If some combatants are unaware. Those aware may take a standard action or a move action (plus free actions). Unaware combatants are flat-footed.
Flat-Footed
Before your first turn, you lose your Dexterity bonus to AC and cannot make attacks of opportunity (unless you have Combat Reflexes). Uncanny dodge prevents this.
Combat Round
A round is 6 seconds; 10 rounds = 1 minute. Effects that last a number of rounds end just before the same initiative count on which they began.
Inaction
Even if you cannot take actions, you retain your initiative score for the duration of the encounter.

33
▾

Escape a grappleStandardNo

Stabilize a dying friendStandardYes

MoveMoveYes

Cease concentrationFreeNo

Prepare spell componentsFreeNo
SpeakFreeNo
5-foot stepNo actionNo
DelayNo actionNo
Perform a combat maneuverVariesYes
Use a featVariesVaries
*Withdraw: only the first square left is safe; moving through additional threatened squares provokes normally. Moving out of a threatened square usually provokes regardless of the action.
Action Details
Standard Action
The most common action. An "attack action" is a specific kind of standard action (e.g., Vital Strike modifies it). Cleave is a standard action but not an attack action.
Move Action
Move your speed, draw a weapon, pick up an item, stand up, direct a spell, or retrieve an item. You can take a move action in place of a standard action.
Full-Round Action
Consumes your entire round. You may take a 5-foot step before, during, or after if the action does not involve movement. Some full-round actions can be taken as standard actions only when you are limited to a single action in the round.
Swift Action
One per turn. Casting a quickened spell or using certain feats. Swift actions are very quick, but you can perform only one swift action per turn.
Immediate Action
Can be used at any time, even when it is not your turn. Counts as your swift action for the next turn. Cannot use while flat-footed.
Free Action
Drop an item, speak, or cease concentration. Reasonable limits apply. You may take free actions while taking other actions.
5-Foot Step
No action. Does not provoke. Only if you have not otherwise moved this round. Cannot be used in difficult terrain or if your speed is 5 ft or less.
Restricted Activity
If limited to a single action, you may take one standard or one move action, plus free and swift actions as normal.
Attack & Damage
8
▾
Melee Attack
BAB + Str modifier + size modifier
Ranged Attack
BAB + Dex modifier + size modifier + range penalty
Natural 1 / Natural 20
A natural 1 on an attack roll is always a miss. A natural 20 is always a hit and a threat (possible critical hit).
Critical Hits
Roll to confirm using the same modifiers. If confirmed, multiply damage (do not multiply precision damage or extra weapon dice).
Strength to Damage
Add your Str modifier to melee and thrown weapon damage. Off-hand weapons add 1/2 Str. Two-handed weapons add 1.5x Str (not light weapons). A penalty (but not a bonus) applies to non-composite bow damage.
Touch Attack
Ignores armor, shield, and natural armor bonuses. Incorporeal touch attacks also ignore cover bonuses (but not force effects like mage armor).
Minimum Damage
If penalties reduce damage below 1, a hit still deals 1 point of nonlethal damage.
Nonlethal Damage
Track separately. When your nonlethal damage equals your current hit points, you are staggered. When it exceeds your current hit points, you fall unconscious.
Breaking Objects
10
▾
Object AC
10 + size mod + Dex (-5 if inanimate, -2 more). Full-round aim = auto-hit melee or +5 ranged.
Hardness
Subtract from damage before reducing HP. Only excess damage counts toward HP loss.
Broken Condition
At ≤ ½ HP. Item is ruined at 0 HP. Damaged items can be repaired with Craft and spells.
Energy vs. Objects
½ damage (divide first, then subtract hardness). Fire → full vs. flammable. Sonic → full vs. glass/crystal.
Ranged vs. Objects
½ damage (except siege engines). Divide first, then apply hardness.
Magic Items
Each +1 enhancement = +2 hardness, +10 HP. Saves: 2 + ½ caster level or owner's, whichever is better.
Object AC by Size
SizeAC ModSizeAC Mod
Colossal-8Small+1
Gargantuan-4Tiny+2
Huge-2Diminutive+4
Large-1Fine+8
Medium+0

SubstanceHardnessHP/inch

Wood510

ActionDCConditionAdjustment
Simple door13Hold portal+5
Good door18Arcane lock+10
Strong door23Oil on hinges+2 circumstance
Rope bonds23
Bend iron bars24
Barred door25

Size modifiers for break checks: Fine -16, Dim -12, Tiny -8, Small -4, Large +4, Huge +8, Gargantuan +12, Colossal +16.
Object & Magic Item Formulas
Combat / Object CalculationFormula or Rule
Object AC10 + size modifier + Dex modifier; inanimate objects have Dex 0 (–5)
Energy damage vs. objectsUsually half damage, then subtract hardness. Fire can deal full damage to vulnerable objects; sonic can deal full damage to glass/crystal.
Ranged weapon damage vs. objectsUsually half damage, then subtract hardness; siege engines are an exception.
Magic item hardnessBase hardness + 2 per +1 enhancement bonus
Magic item hit pointsBase HP + 10 per +1 enhancement bonus
Magic item saves2 + 1/2 caster level, or the owner’s save bonus if better
Armor & Defense
4
▾
Armor Class
10 + armor + shield + Dex + natural + deflection + dodge + size + misc
Flat-Footed
You lose your Dexterity bonus to AC and cannot make attacks of opportunity. Uncanny dodge prevents losing Dex.
Touch AC
Does not include armor, shield, or natural armor bonuses. All other modifiers apply normally.
Dodge Bonuses
Stack with each other. Lost whenever you lose your Dex bonus to AC (unlike armor, which does not limit dodge).

20
▾

SituationMelee AtkRanged Atk

Conditions
22
▾
Bleed
Take listed damage at start of turn. Removed via DC 15 Heal check or healing spell.
Blinded
-2 AC, lose Dex to AC, -4 Str/Dex skills. Foes have total concealment vs you.
Broken
Weapons -2 atk/dmg, crits on natural 20 only. Armor/shields give half AC.
Confused
Roll d100 each round: 01-25 act normally, 26-50 babble 1 round, 51-75 deal 1d8+Str to self, 76-100 attack nearest creature.
Dazed
No actions. Does not lose Dex to AC.
Entangled
No movement if anchored, else half speed. -2 atk, -4 Dex. Concentration checks to cast.
Exhausted
Half speed. -6 Str and Dex. Rest 1 hour → Fatigued.
Fatigued
Cannot run or charge. -2 Str and Dex. Rest 8 hrs to recover.
Frightened
As Shaken + must flee if possible.
Grappled
Cannot move or use two-handed actions. -4 Dex, -2 atk/CMB (except escape). No AoOs.
Helpless
Paralyzed, held, bound, sleeping, or at mercy of others. Dex = 0. Coup de grace possible.
Incorporeal
No physical body. Immune to nonmagical attacks. Half damage from magic (full from force/incorporeal).
Invisible
+2 atk, +20 Stealth. Defenders lose Dex bonus to AC against you.
Nauseated
Can only move. No attacks, spellcasts, or concentration.
Panicked
As Frightened. Drop held items, run from all dangers.
Paralyzed
Str and Dex = 0. Winged fliers fall. Helpless.
Pinned
As Grappled + Flat-Footed, -4 AC, only verbal/mental actions (except escape).
Prone
-4 melee atk. No ranged weapons except crossbows. +4 AC vs ranged, -4 AC vs melee.
Shaken
-2 atk, weapon damage, saves, skill checks, ability checks.
Sickened
-2 atk, weapon damage, saves, skill checks, ability checks.
Staggered
Only 1 Move or Standard action per turn (plus Free, Swift, Immediate).
Stunned
Cannot act. Drops everything. -2 AC. Lose Dex bonus to AC.
Combat Maneuvers
10
▾
CMB
BAB + Str modifier + special size modifier. Tiny and smaller creatures use Dex instead of Str.
CMD
10 + BAB + Str + Dex + special size modifier + misc. Misc includes deflection, dodge, insight, luck, morale, profane, sacred bonuses to AC. Flat-footed does not add Dex.
Success
Roll d20 + CMB vs target CMD. Natural 20 always succeeds; natural 1 always fails. If hit by an AoO while performing a maneuver, take damage as a penalty to the CMB check.
AoO
Unless you have the Improved feat for that maneuver, initiating it provokes an attack of opportunity from the target.
Special Size Modifiers
SizeCMB / CMDSizeCMB / CMD
Fine-8Large+1
Diminutive-4Huge+2
Tiny-2Gargantuan+4
Small-1Colossal+8
Medium+0
Maneuver Effects
Bull Rush
Push target 5 ft straight back, +5 ft per 5 by which you exceed CMD. You can move with the target. Standard or part of a charge. Max 1 size larger.
Dirty Trick
Impose blinded, dazzled, deafened, entangled, shaken, or sickened for 1 round (+1 round per 5 over CMD). Target can remove the condition as a move action; Greater Dirty Trick increases the removal to a standard action.
Disarm
Target drops 1 item (2 items if exceed CMD by 10). If you fail by 10, you drop your weapon. Unarmed attempt takes -4 penalty.
Drag
Pull target 5 ft, +5 ft per 5 over CMD. Both you and target move. Standard. Max 1 size larger.
Grapple
Requires at least one free hand; attempting without two free hands takes a -4 penalty. Both gain grappled. Maintain as a standard action with +5 on subsequent checks. Options: move, damage, pin, or tie up. Escape with CMB or Escape Artist vs CMD.
Overrun
Move through target's square. Standard or charge. Target can avoid; if not, check vs CMD. Exceed by 5 = target knocked prone. +2 CMD per extra leg beyond 2.
Reposition
Move target 5 ft, +5 ft per 5 over CMD. Must remain within your reach except the final 5 ft. Standard. Max 1 size larger.
Steal
Take an item not held or hidden. Fastened items grant +5 CMD bonus. Standard. One hand must be free. Max 1 size larger.
Sunder
Deal damage to an item. Broken at ≤ 1/2 HP. You can choose to destroy it if damage would reduce it below 0.
Trip
Knock the target prone if your check equals or exceeds CMD. If you fail by 10 or more, you fall prone. Defenders gain +2 CMD per extra leg. You cannot trip creatures without legs or flying creatures.
Special Attacks
9
▾
Aid Another
Attack vs AC 10. Success grants ally +2 on next attack or +2 AC vs that foe. Multiple aids stack. Standard action.
Charge
Full-round. Move up to 2x speed in a straight line to the closest attack square. +2 attack, -2 AC until next turn. Must have line of sight. No 5-ft step. Lances deal double damage when mounted.
Feint
Bluff vs 10 + BAB + Wis (or Sense Motive if higher). Success denies target Dex to AC against your next melee attack. Standard. Move action with Improved Feint. -4 non-humanoid, -8 animal Int 1-2, impossible vs no Int.
Withdraw
Full-round. Move up to 2x speed. The first square left is not threatened by visible foes. No 5-ft step this round.
Ready
Standard action. Specify action and trigger. Occurs before the trigger. Your initiative moves to just before the triggering action. If you take it before your next turn, you lose your regular action that round.
Delay
No action. Act later at a lower initiative count. If you delay until after your next turn would have begun, you lose the delayed action for that round.
Coup de Grace
Full-round. Auto-hit and critical hit. Target must Fort save DC 10 + damage dealt or die. Provokes AoO. Cannot use on creatures immune to critical hits.
Throw Splash Weapon
Ranged touch attack. Miss = 1d8 misdirection per range increment. Splash damage to all adjacent squares. Targeting an intersection is AC 5.
Two-Weapon Fighting Penalties
CircumstancesPrimary HandOff Hand
Normal penalties-6-10
Off-hand weapon is light-4-8
Two-Weapon Fighting feat-4-4
Light off-hand + TWF feat-2-2
An unarmed strike is always considered light. Penalties apply only to the full-attack action; AoOs are at normal bonuses.
Mounted Combat
8
▾
At a Glance
Two Creatures
The rider and mount are separate creatures. Attacks, saves, hit points, and conditions apply to whichever creature is actually targeted or affected.
Same Initiative
Your mount acts on your initiative count as you direct it. You move at its speed, but the mount uses its action to move.
Shared Space
For simplicity, assume the rider shares the mount’s space in combat. A horse is Large and occupies a 10-ft. space.
Combat Training
Untrained mounts are frightened by combat; controlling one in battle requires Ride DC 20 as a move action each round.
Shared Space & Targeting
Choosing a Target
An attacker chooses a legal target: the rider or the mount. Use that target’s AC, defenses, hit points, and conditions.
Area Effects
If both rider and mount are in an area effect, resolve the effect against each creature separately.
No Assigned Rider Square
PF1 gives a shared-space simplification and does not assign the rider to a specific square on the mount. Apply that shared-space rule consistently for targeting, adjacency, and reach questions.
Mount as Cover
Ride DC 15 as an immediate action lets the rider drop down beside the mount and use it as cover. The rider cannot attack or cast spells while using the mount as cover; recovering is a move action.
Reach & Threat
Rider Reach
The rider’s reach does not change, but it is measured from the shared mounted space. With normal 5-ft. melee reach, the rider threatens adjacent squares around the mount’s space.
Reach Weapons
Reach weapons follow their normal rules from the shared mounted space. A typical reach weapon threatens farther squares but not adjacent squares.
Mount Reach
The mount threatens separately with its own natural reach and attacks. A horse or similar Large long creature usually threatens adjacent squares with its natural attacks.
Higher Ground
When the rider attacks a creature smaller than the mount that is on foot, the rider gains the +1 melee attack bonus for being on higher ground.
Rules note: mounted reach can create awkward edge cases because the official rule is “share your mount’s space,” not “choose a square on your mount.”
Attacks of Opportunity
Movement
Movement by the mount can provoke normally when the mounted pair leaves threatened squares. The attack of opportunity is resolved against one declared legal target the attacker threatens.
Rider or Mount?
Because rider and mount are separate creatures, an attacker that can legally attack either must choose which creature the AoO targets. Attacking both requires enough attacks of opportunity.
Casting & Ranged Attacks
A mounted rider still provokes normally for actions such as casting a spell, making a ranged attack, reading a scroll, drinking a potion, or similar provoking actions.
Control Checks
Controlling an untrained mount in battle is a move action and can interact with movement-based provoking as normal.
Controlling a Mount
Combat-Trained Mount
Horses, ponies, and riding dogs can serve readily as combat steeds. A combat-trained mount can act in battle without the DC 20 control check required for an untrained mount.
Untrained Mount
A mount without combat training is frightened by combat. If you do not dismount, make Ride DC 20 each round as a move action to control it.
Success / Failure
On success, you may still take a standard action after the move action. On failure, that move action becomes a full-round action and you can do nothing else until your next turn.
Guide with Knees
Ride DC 5 lets you guide with your knees so both hands remain free. In the Ride skill this check is made at the start of your turn and takes no action.
Animal Companions & Combat Mounts
Combat Riding Purpose
Handle Animal DC 20 trains an animal for combat riding: attack, come, defend, down, guard, and heel. Training takes 6 weeks.
Upgrade Riding Training
An animal trained for riding can be upgraded to combat riding with 3 weeks of work and a successful DC 20 Handle Animal check. The new purpose replaces the prior purpose and tricks.
Handle / Push
Commanding a known trick is a move action; pushing an unknown but physically possible trick is a full-round action. If wounded or damaged, increase the Handle Animal DC by 2.
Animal Companions
A druid or ranger handles her animal companion as a free action or pushes it as a move action, gains +4 on Handle Animal checks involving it, and receives bonus tricks that do not count against the normal limit.
Attack Trick Limits
Attack normally covers humanoids, monstrous humanoids, giants, and animals. Teaching an animal to attack all creature types counts as two tricks.
Defend Trick
An animal with defend protects you, or a specific creature you designate, even without an additional command if no threat is present.
Ride Checks & Defensive Options
TaskRide DCAction / Use
Guide with knees5No action at start of turn
Stay in saddle5No action; used if mount rears/bolts or rider takes damage
Fight with combat-trained mount10Free action; mount attacks and rider still attacks normally
Use mount as cover15Immediate action; recover as a move action
Soft fall if mount falls15No action; failure means 1d6 damage and prone
Control mount in battle20Move action; only for mounts not trained for combat riding
Fast mount or dismount20Free action if you still have a move action available; otherwise move action
Mounted Combat Feat
Once per round, when your mount is hit, you may spend an immediate action to make a Ride check. If your Ride result exceeds the opponent’s attack roll, the hit on the mount is negated.
Unconscious Rider
If you are knocked unconscious, you have a 50% chance to stay in the saddle, or 75% in a military saddle. Otherwise you fall and take 1d6 damage.
Mounted Attacks & Charging
Melee After Movement
If your mount moves more than 5 feet, you can make only a single melee attack. You cannot make a full attack with melee weapons after that movement.
Mount Attacks Too
Ride DC 10 to fight with a combat-trained mount lets the mount attack in battle while the rider still makes his own attack or attacks normally.
Mounted Charge
If the mount charges, the rider also takes the charge AC penalty. If the rider attacks at the end of the charge, the rider gains the charge attack bonus.
Lance
A lance deals double damage when used by a mounted character in a charge.
Mounted Ranged Attacks
Ranged attacks while the mount double moves take a –4 penalty; while the mount runs, –8. The attack is made when the mount has completed half its movement.
Mounted Ranged Full Attack
You can make a full attack with a ranged weapon while your mount is moving, and you can still take move actions normally.
Spellcasting, Falling & Recovery
Normal Movement
You can cast normally if the mount moves up to its speed either before or after you cast.
Vigorous Motion
If the mount moves both before and after you cast, make a concentration check DC 10 + spell level or lose the spell.
Violent Motion
If the mount is running, you can cast when it has moved up to twice its speed, but the concentration DC is 15 + spell level.
If the Mount Falls
If your mount falls, Ride DC 15 lets you make a soft fall and take no damage. On failure, you take 1d6 damage.
Without a Rider
If the rider is unconscious or absent, the mount avoids combat unless trained/commanded otherwise.
Recover from Cover
If you used the mount as cover, returning to the normal riding position is a move action and requires no check.
Injury, Death & Healing
7
▾
0 HP
Disabled. Staggered. You may take a standard or move action (not both). Any strenuous action deals 1 damage, dropping you to -1 and dying.
Negative HP
Unconscious and dying. Each turn, attempt a DC 10 Constitution check to stabilize; failure means you lose 1 hit point.
Stabilize
While dying, attempt a DC 10 Constitution check on your turn, with a penalty equal to your negative hit point total. A natural 20 automatically succeeds. A DC 15 Heal check can also stabilize you.
Dead
When negative HP reaches your Constitution score (or lower), or from massive damage.
Recovery with Help
After 1 hour, DC 10 Con check to wake (penalty = negative HP). Natural 20 auto-succeeds. Unconscious creatures still heal naturally.
Recovery without Help
Each failed Con check = lose 1 HP. No natural healing. Once conscious, DC 10 Con check after 8 hrs rest to begin recovering naturally.
Natural Healing
8 hours rest = 1 HP per level. 24 hours bed rest = 2 HP per level. Significant interruptions prevent healing.
Magical Healing
Restores HP up to your maximum. Also removes an equal amount of nonlethal damage.
Temporary Hit Points
Lost before real HP. Do not stack from the same source. Stack from different sources. Cannot be restored.
Nonlethal Damage
Heal at 1 HP per hour per character level. When it equals current HP, you are staggered. When it exceeds current HP, you fall unconscious.
Spellcasting in Combat
13
▾
Components
Verbal: cannot speak if gagged or in a silence spell. Deafened = 20% spell failure for verbal spells. Somatic: need one free hand; cannot if bound/grappling. Material/Focus: preparing components is a free action unless they are elaborate.
Casting Defensively
Concentration check DC 15 + 2 × spell level. Success means no AoO. Failure means the spell is lost.
Concentration Checks
If injured while casting: DC 10 + damage dealt + spell level. Continuous damage: 10 + ½ damage + SL. Non-damaging spell: DC of that spell + SL. Entangled: 15 + SL. Grappled: 10 + grappler CMB + SL. Vigorous motion: 10 + SL. Violent motion: 15 + SL.
Touch Spells
Casting + delivery in the same round is a free action. You may hold the charge indefinitely. Casting another spell dissipates it. Touch 1 friend automatically; up to 6 friends as a full-round action.
Ranged Touch Spells
The ranged touch attack is made as part of the spell and provokes an AoO even if the spell was cast defensively. Unless noted otherwise, it cannot be held for a later turn.
Metamagic Timing
For sorcerers/bards, a metamagic spell with normal casting time 1 standard action becomes a full-round action (except Quicken). For clerics/druids, spontaneous metamagic cure/inflict/summon spells are full-round actions.
Concentration Check DCs
SituationConcentration DC
Casting defensively15 + double spell level
Injured while casting10 + damage dealt + spell level
Continuous damage while casting10 + 1/2 damage dealt + spell level
Affected by a non-damaging spellDC of the disrupting spell + spell level
Entangled while casting15 + spell level
Grappled or pinned while casting10 + grappler's CMB + spell level
Vigorous motion, vigorous wind, or rain/sleet10 + spell level
Violent motion or violent wind15 + spell level
Extremely violent motion, wind, or weather20 + spell level
Tactical Movement & Position
6
▾
Tactical Speed by Race
RaceNo Armor / LightMedium or Heavy
Human, Elf, Half-Elf, Half-Orc30 ft. (6 sq)20 ft. (4 sq)
Dwarf20 ft. (4 sq)20 ft. (4 sq)
Halfling, Gnome20 ft. (4 sq)15 ft. (3 sq)
Diagonals
1st diagonal = 1 sq. 2nd = 2 sq. 3rd = 1 sq. 4th = 2 sq. You cannot move diagonally past a corner.
Difficult Terrain
Each square counts as 2 squares. You cannot run or charge through difficult terrain.
Squeezing
Move into a space at least half your width. Each square counts as 2. -4 on attack rolls and -4 to AC while squeezed.
Reach
Medium creatures have 5 ft reach. Large (tall) = 10 ft. Huge (tall) = 15 ft, etc. Creatures with natural reach > 5 ft still threaten adjacent squares. Reach weapons double natural reach but do not threaten adjacent squares.
Big & Little Creatures
Fine/Dim/Tiny creatures enter an opponent's square to attack (provokes AoO). Tiny = 2.5 ft space. Large = 10 ft. Huge = 15 ft. Gargantuan = 20 ft. Colossal = 30 ft.
Double Movement Costs
If doubled twice, each square = 4 squares (or 6 diagonally). If doubled three times, each square = 8 squares (12 diagonally). This is an exception to the normal doubling rule.
Use search to find and open matching skills. Each skill card expands into source-derived details from Skill Checks.md.
No skills match.
Exploration & Way Signs
5
▾
Discovery Points
Accumulated by succeeding on exploration checks or interpreting way signs. Spent to discover locations. Persist indefinitely per territory.
Exploration Check
Usually Survival; can use Perception at -5. DC determined by territory CR. Success = 1 DP + 1 per 5 over DC.
Seeking a Location
Spend DP equal to the location's discovery score. For unknown locations: spend DP, divide by 2, compare to all location scores.
Documenting
Profession (cartographer) or Linguistics. # checks = territory CR; DC = exploration DC. Success grants +5 circumstance bonus on future checks.
Territory CR → Exploration DC
CRDCCRDCCRDC
1178261533
2199271634
32110281735
42211291836
52312301937
62413312038
7251432
Way Signs
ComplexityDCDP RewardFailure Penalty
SimpleCR + 101—
ModerateCR + 153—
ComplexCR + 205Lose 1d4 DP on fail by 5+
Discovery Score Modifiers
ConditionModifier
Desert or plains terrain+1
Forest, hills, or marsh terrain+2
Mountain terrain+3
Location traveled to/from often-4
Location is mobile+4
Unusually large-2
Unusually small+2
Deliberately hidden+2 to +6
Base scores: Obvious = 3, Obscure = 6. Minimum = 1. Adjustments are cumulative.
Modifiers & Rewards
Careful Exploration
Extra check/day, but max DP gain = 1 (no bonus for exceeding DC).
Darkness (≤ ½ night)
-4 on exploration checks without darkvision.
Darkness (> ½ night)
-8 on exploration checks without darkvision.
Extend Day (+8 hrs)
Cumulative -4 per extra 8-hr block; may cause fatigue/exhaustion.
Light Fog / Rain
-1 on exploration checks.
Blizzard / Hurricane
-10 on exploration checks.
Experience Points
XP equal to defeating a monster of the territory's CR upon discovering all locations.
Map / Gazetteer Sale
First sale = 100 × CR gp. Subsequent copies ≈ 10% of that value.
Overland Travel
7
▾
Overland Travel Overview
Core Use
This section consolidates travel pace, terrain, forced march, vehicles, mounts, and encumbrance in one place.
Quick Rule
Travel is usually measured in 8-hour days. Terrain, armor/load, visibility, forced march, and vehicle propulsion can all adjust the practical distance covered.
Movement Rates
Walk
Unhurried movement: 3 mph (unencumbered human). 8 hrs/day standard.
Hustle
Jog: 6 mph. 1 hr OK; 2nd hr → 1 nonlethal + fatigued. Each additional hr doubles damage.
Run (×3)
Heavy armor running pace (~7 mph for human in full plate).
Run (×4)
Light/medium/no armor running pace (~12 mph unencumbered, ~9 mph in chainmail).
Tactical & Overland Distances
SpeedWalk/RndHustle/RndRun×3Run×4Walk/HrWalk/Day
15 ft153045601½ mi12 mi
20 ft204060802 mi16 mi
30 ft3060901203 mi24 mi
40 ft40801201604 mi32 mi
Day = 8 hrs travel. Tactical = feet per round. Local = feet per minute. Overland = miles per hour/day.
Terrain & Hampered Movement
Terrain Multipliers (Overland)
TerrainHighwayRoad/TrailTrackless
Desert, Sandy×1×½×½
Forest×1×1×½
Hills×1×¾×½
Jungle×1×¾×¼
Moor×1×1×¾
Mountains×¾×¾×½
Plains×1×1×¾
Swamp×1×¾×½
Tundra, Frozen×1×¾×¾
Hampered Movement
ConditionCost MultiplierNotes
Difficult terrain×2Each square counts as 2
Obstacle*×2May require skill check
Poor visibility×2Fog, darkness, etc.
Impassable—Cannot enter
Multiple hampering conditions multiply together (exception to normal doubling rule). Cannot run or charge through hampered squares.
Forced March & Fatigue
Forced March & Fatigue
Forced March DC
Beyond 8 hrs walking: DC 10 + 2 per extra hour. Con check fail → 1d6 nonlethal + fatigued.
Fatigued Condition
-2 Str, -2 Dex, cannot run or charge. Removing nonlethal damage removes fatigue.
Mounted Hustle
Lethal damage (not nonlethal). Mounted forced march → auto-fail Con checks, lethal damage.
Evasion & Pursuit
Short chase: opposed Dex checks. Long chase: opposed Con checks. Higher roll wins.
Reduced Speed
Reduced Speed (Medium/Heavy Load or Armor)
Base SpeedReduced SpeedBase SpeedReduced Speed
5 ft5 ft65 ft45 ft
10–15 ft10 ft70–75 ft50 ft
20 ft15 ft80 ft55 ft
25–30 ft20 ft85–90 ft60 ft
35 ft25 ft95 ft65 ft
40–45 ft30 ft100–105 ft70 ft
50 ft35 ft110 ft75 ft
55–60 ft40 ft115–120 ft80 ft
Mounts & Vehicles
Mount & Vehicle Speeds
Mount / VehiclePer HourPer Day
Light horse5 mi40 mi
Light horse (175–525 lbs.)3½ mi28 mi
Heavy horse5 mi40 mi
Heavy horse (229–690 lbs.)3½ mi28 mi
Pony4 mi32 mi
Pony (151–450 lbs.)3 mi24 mi

Riding dog (101–300 lbs.)3 mi24 mi

Keelboat (rowed)1 mi10 mi
Rowboat (rowed)1½ mi15 mi
Sailing ship2 mi48 mi
Warship2½ mi60 mi
Longship3 mi72 mi

Downstream: add current speed (~3 mph). Ship day = 24 hrs; rowed vessel day = 10 hrs.
Vehicle Stat Blocks (Ultimate Combat)
VehicleCostSizeACHard/HPPropulsion
Cart15 gp———Pulled
Chariot, Light50 gp5×10 ft95/302 Med / 1 Lg
Chariot, Medium100 gp5×10 ft85/504 Med / 1 Lg
Chariot, Heavy200 gp10×15 ft85/806 Med / 2 Lg
Carriage100 gp10×15 ft95/906 Med / 2 Lg
Wagon, Light35 gp10×10 ft95/604 Med / 1 Lg
Wagon, Medium50 gp10×15 ft95/906 Med / 2 Lg
Wagon, Heavy100 gp15×20 ft85/1208 Med / 4 Lg
All wood (Hardness 5). Max speed = 2× pulling creature speed. Acceleration: chariots = creature speed, wagons = ½ creature speed.
Vehicle Details
Cart
Weight: 200 lbs empty. Capacity: 300 lbs cargo.
Carriage
4 passengers in enclosed cab + driver + 1 armed passenger. Roof cargo: 200 lbs.
Chariot, Light
Open platform, racing or single archer. Ram: 1d8. CMB +1 / CMD 11.
Chariot, Medium
Open platform with 1 scythe weapon. Ram: 1d8. CMB +1 / CMD 11.
Chariot, Heavy
Fighting platform with light ballista. Ram: 1d8. CMB +2 / CMD 12.
Wagon, Light
400 lbs cargo. Ram: 1d8. CMB +2 / CMD 12. Base Save +0.
Wagon, Medium
2,000 lbs cargo. Ram: 2d8. CMB +2 / CMD 12. Base Save +1.
Wagon, Heavy
4,000 lbs cargo. Ram: 2d8. CMB +2 / CMD 12. Base Save +2. Size: Huge (12 squares).
Vehicle Combat & Crews
Driving Actions
Accelerate (std), Decelerate (std), Keep Going (move), Turn (std), Reverse (std). Driver acts first or vehicle becomes uncontrolled.
Crew Requirements
Full crew = normal. Half crew = +10 DC. Below half = cannot drive. Crew takes no actions while moving.
Cover
Vehicles grant partial cover: +2 AC, +1 Reflex saves. Armored vehicles may grant more.
Deceleration Coast
When speed reaches 0, vehicle coasts 1d4 × 5 ft forward before stopping.
Carrying Capacity & Encumbrance
Encumbrance Effects
LoadMax DexCheck PenaltySpeed (30 ft base)Run
Light——30 ft×4
Medium+3-320 ft×4
Heavy+1-620 ft×3
Use the worse figure from armor or load — do not stack penalties. Light load = no encumbrance.
Carrying Capacity by Strength (lbs.)
StrLightMediumHeavyModExample
10–34–67–10-5
20–67–1314–20-4
30–1011–2021–30-4
40–1314–2627–40-3
50–1617–3334–50-3
60–2021–4041–60-2
70–2324–4647–70-2
80–2627–5354–80-1
90–3031–6061–90-1
100–3334–6667–100+0Average human
110–3839–7677–115+0
120–4344–8687–130+1

180–100101–200201–300+4

250–266267–533534–800+7

270–346347–693694–1,040+8
280–400401–800801–1,200+9
290–466467–933934–1,400+9
300–532533–1,0661,067–1,600+10Fire giant
For Str 30+: find the Str 20–29 row with the same "ones" digit, multiply values by 4 for every 10 points above that row.
Size & Type Multipliers
Bipedal Creatures
Fine ×⅛, Diminutive ×¼, Tiny ×½, Small ×¾, Medium ×1, Large ×2, Huge ×4, Gargantuan ×8, Colossal ×16.
Quadrupeds
Fine ×¼, Diminutive ×½, Tiny ×¾, Small ×1, Medium ×1½, Large ×3, Huge ×6, Gargantuan ×12, Colossal ×24.
Lifting
Max load overhead. Double max load off ground (move 5 ft/round as full-round, lose Dex to AC).
Push / Drag
5× max load on ground. Favorable conditions can double; bad conditions halve or more.
Travel Formulas
Travel Formulas
CalculationRule
Forced march DCDC 10 + 2 per extra hour beyond 8 hours
Way sign DCsSimple CR + 10; Moderate CR + 15; Complex CR + 20
Map / gazetteer sale100 × territory CR gp for the first sale; later copies usually about 10%
Pulled vehicle max speed2 × pulling creature’s speed
Mixed propulsionFastest propulsion + 1/2 second-fastest propulsion
Vehicle decelerationWhen speed reaches 0, coast 1d4 × 5 ft before stopping
Encumbrance Speed
Use the worse of armor or load effects; do not stack both sets of penalties.
Hampered Movement
When multiple hampering conditions apply, multiply movement costs together; this is an exception to normal doubling.
Ship vs. Rowed Vessel Days
Ship overland day generally assumes 24 hours of travel; rowed vessel day generally assumes 10 hours.
Mounted Damage from Hustle
A mount that hustles too long takes lethal damage, not nonlethal damage.
Goods, Services & Settlement Economy
6
▾
At a Glance
Base Value
There is a 75% chance that any item at or below a settlement’s base value is available for sale. If unavailable, check again in 1 week.
Purchase Limit
The most money a merchant in that settlement can spend to buy any single item from the PCs.
Spellcasting
Spellcasting for hire is limited by settlement size separately from magic item base value.
Above Base Value
Magic items above base value are available only if generated as part of the settlement’s current magic item stock.
Settlement Statistics
SettlementPopulationModQual.DangerBase ValuePurchase LimitSpellcasting
ThorpFewer than 20–41–1050 gp500 gp1st
Hamlet21–60–21–5200 gp1,000 gp2nd
Village61–200–120500 gp2,500 gp3rd
Small Town201–2,0000201,000 gp5,000 gp4th
Large Town2,001–5,0000352,000 gp10,000 gp5th
Small City5,001–10,000+1454,000 gp25,000 gp6th
Large City10,001–25,000+25108,000 gp50,000 gp7th
MetropolisMore than 25,000+461016,000 gp100,000 gp8th
Modifiers
Apply to local Appraise, Bluff, Diplomacy, and Knowledge (local) checks in the settlement.
Danger
Applies to Initiative checks and Perception checks made to avoid being surprised by hazards or urban threats.
Magic Item Availability
Magic Item Stock Above Base Value
SettlementBase ValueMinorMediumMajor
Thorp50 gp1d4——
Hamlet200 gp1d6——
Village500 gp2d41d4—
Small Town1,000 gp3d41d6—
Large Town2,000 gp3d42d41d4
Small City4,000 gp4d43d41d6
Large City8,000 gp4d43d42d4
Metropolis16,000 gpVirtually all4d43d4
If a random item result is below the settlement’s base value, reroll until the result exceeds base value.
For the exact item category and item results, use the standard magic item generation tables. If a generated item is below the settlement’s base value, reroll until the result exceeds base value.
Consumable Pricing
Scroll Price
spell level × caster level × 25 gp + component cost. 0-level spells count as 1/2 level.
Potion / Oil Price
spell level × caster level × 50 gp + component cost. Only qualifying spells of 3rd level or lower can be potions/oils.
Standard Scroll Prices
Spell LevelCLPrice
0-level112.5 gp
1st125 gp
2nd3150 gp
3rd5375 gp
4th7700 gp
5th91,125 gp
6th111,650 gp
7th132,275 gp
8th153,000 gp
9th173,825 gp
Standard Potion/Oil Prices
Spell LevelCLPrice
0-level125 gp
1st150 gp
2nd3300 gp
3rd5750 gp
Spellcasting Services
Fee
spell level × caster level × 10 gp + material/focus costs. 0-level spells count as 1/2 level, so the base fee is 5 gp.
3,000 gp Limit
If the total service fee, including expensive components, exceeds 3,000 gp, the spell is generally not available for purchase.
Components
The buyer pays consumed material component costs; expensive focus requirements may require the buyer to provide the focus.
Transport Return
Long-distance transport spells such as teleport usually require paying for a return casting as well.
Hirelings & Services
Common Services
ServicePriceServicePrice
Road or gate toll1 cpMessenger2 cp/mile
Inn stay, poor2 sp/nightBath, cold2 cp
Inn stay, common5 sp/nightBath, hot6 cp
Inn stay, good2 gp/nightBath, public2 sp–1 gp
Inn suite, small4 gpLaundry1 sp
Inn suite, average16 gpMagic laundry1 gp
Inn suite, luxurious32 gp+Fence10% goods value
Hireling Rates
HirelingCostHirelingCost
Untrained laborer1–2 sp/dayFootman5 sp/day
Trained hireling3 sp+/dayNurse7 sp/day
Valet / butler / steward1 gp/dayDoctor1 gp/day
Lawyer, novice5 spLawyer, experienced10 gp
Scribe, text1 gpScribe, map10 gp
Chronicler5 gp/daySage15 gp/day
Finding Higher-Level Hirelings
Base Check
In a small town or larger, Diplomacy DC 15 locates 1st-level commoners, experts, or warriors.
Level Modifier
Increase the DC by +5 per level above 1st.
Rare / PC Class
Add +5 DC for a PC class or rare skill set, such as rogue, fighter, cleric, or specialist work.
Level Cap
Highest available hireling level is usually capped at twice the settlement’s highest available spellcasting level.
Success
Success locates 1d6 skeptical or 2d6 wary hirelings.
Success by 5+
Adds another 1d6 hirelings or increases the starting Trust of the hirelings by +1.
Vision & Light
2
▾
Illumination Levels
Bright Light
Cannot use Stealth unless invisible/in cover. Light-sensitive creatures are dazzled. Light-blind are blinded.
Normal Light
No penalties. Forest canopy, 20 ft from torch, light spell area.
Dim Light
Concealment (20% miss chance) vs. those without darkvision. Can use Stealth. Moonlit night, 20–40 ft from torch.
Darkness
Without darkvision = effectively blind (50% miss chance, lose Dex to AC, -2 AC, -4 Perception). Darkvision sees grayscale up to 60 ft.
Light Sources
SourceRadiusIncreasedDuration
Candle— (dim only)5 ft1 hr
Torch20 ft40 ft1 hr
Lamp, common15 ft30 ft6 hr/pint
Lantern, hooded30 ft60 ft6 hr/pint
Lantern, bullseye60-ft cone120-ft cone6 hr/pint
Sunrod30 ft60 ft6 hr
Light spell20 ft40 ft10 min/level
Continual flame20 ft40 ftPermanent
Daylight60 ft120 ft10 min/level
Low-light vision: double all radii. Darkvision: see in dark up to 60 ft (grayscale).

Pathfinder 1e Player Quick Reference

Consolidated alternate view for table use. Detailed tabs remain the source for edge cases.

1 Round = Standard Action + Move Action + Swift Action
or Immediate + Free Actions or Full-Round Action
instead of Standard + Move
You may take a move action instead of a standard action. An immediate action used outside your turn consumes your next turn’s swift action.

Offense

ActionCostAoO?
Attack (melee)StandardNo
Attack (ranged)StandardYes
Attack (unarmed)StandardYes
Full attackFull-roundNo
Attack of opportunityNo actionNo
ChargeFull-round2Move3
Perform combat maneuverVaries1Usually
Deliver coup de graceFull-roundYes

Escape a grappleStandardNo

Extinguish flamesFull-roundNo

RunFull-roundYes3
Take 5-foot stepNo action5No
Drop proneFreeNo

DelayNo actionNo

Acrobatics to avoid AoOs while movingNo actionNo
Bluff to feintStandardNo
Handle Animal: known trickMove6No
Handle Animal: push unknown trickFull-round6No
Heal to stabilize / remove bleedStandardYes
Intimidate to demoralizeStandardNo
Knowledge to identify creatureNo actionNo

Spellcraft to identify spellNo actionNo

ActionCostAoO?

Touch spell after casting roundStandard8No
Touch up to six willing alliesFull-roundYes

Extraordinary abilityStandardNo
Spell-like abilityStandardYes
Supernatural abilityStandardNo
Concentrate to maintain spellStandardNo

Direct / redirect active spellMoveNo

Items & Objects

ActionCostAoO?
Drop an itemFreeNo
Pick up an itemMoveYes
Retrieve stored itemMoveYes
Draw a weaponMove9No
Draw hidden weaponStandardNo
Sheathe weaponMoveYes
Ready / drop shieldMove9No
Load hand or light crossbowMoveYes
Load heavy or repeating crossbowFull-roundYes
Nock arrow to bowFreeNo
Prepare splash weaponFull-roundYes
Activate magic item other than potion/oilStandardUsually no
Drink potion / apply oilStandardYes
Read scrollStandardYes
Light torch with tindertwigStandardYes
Move a heavy objectMoveYes
Open / close doorMoveNo

Miscellaneous

ActionCostAoO?
Aid anotherStandardMaybe10
SpeakFree11No
Use a featVariesVaries
1. Some maneuvers replace melee attacks and can be used during attacks, full attacks, charges, or AoOs; others require a specific action. Most provoke unless an ability says otherwise.
2. May be taken as a standard action if you are limited to only a single action in the round.
3. Moving out of a threatened square usually provokes regardless of the action being used.
4. Withdraw makes only the first square you leave not threatened by visible foes; later threatened squares can still provoke.
5. You cannot 5-foot step if you otherwise move this round or in difficult terrain.
6. A druid/ranger can handle an animal companion as a free action or push it as a move action.
7. A 1-round casting-time spell completes just before your next turn.
8. Casting a touch spell may provoke; the touch attack itself is armed and does not. Ranged touch attacks cannot normally be held.
9. With BAB +1, draw/ready can be combined with regular movement; Two-Weapon Fighting allows two light/one-handed weapons to be drawn in the same time.
10. Aid another provokes if the action being aided would normally provoke.
11. Speaking more than a few sentences is beyond a free action.

Attack & Armor Class Modifiers

Attacker is…MeleeRanged
Dazzled-1-1
Entangled-2-2
Flanking defender+2—
Invisible+2+2
On higher ground+1+0

Concentration & Spellcasting

SituationConcentration DC
Casting defensively15 + double spell level
Injured while casting10 + damage dealt + spell level
Continuous damage10 + 1/2 damage dealt + spell level
Affected by non-damaging spellDisrupting spell DC + spell level
Entangled while casting15 + spell level
Grappled / pinned while casting10 + grappler’s CMB + spell level
Vigorous motion / wind / debris10 + spell level
Violent motion / violent wind15 + spell level

Combat Maneuvers

ManeuverEffect
Bull RushPush 5 ft, +5 ft per 5 by which check exceeds CMD.
Dirty TrickInflict listed condition for 1 round, +1 round per 5 over CMD.
DisarmTarget drops item; two items if check exceeds CMD by 10. Fail by 10: you drop weapon.
DragPull 5 ft, +5 ft per 5 over CMD.
GrappleAt least one free hand; -4 without two free hands. Maintain +5; move, damage, pin, or tie up.
OverrunMove through target; exceed CMD by 5 to knock prone.
RepositionMove target 5 ft, +5 ft per 5 over CMD.
StealTake item not held or hidden; one hand free.
SunderDamage item; broken at half hp, destroyed at 0 hp.
TripKnock target prone. Fail by 10: you fall prone.

Concealment & Cover

ConcealmentMissAoO?Stealth?
Concealment20%YesYes
Total concealment50%NoYes
CoverACRefStealth
Cover+4+2Yes
Partial cover+2+1Yes
Improved cover+8+4+10
Total coverCannot be targeted; +10 Stealth
Soft cover+4+0No

Common Conditions

Bleed: Take listed damage at start of turn; same damage types stack, different types stack. DC 15 Heal or healing spell removes.

Blinded: -2 AC, lose Dex to AC, -4 Str/Dex skill checks and opposed Perception; opponents have total concealment.

Broken: Weapons -2 attack/damage, crit only on natural 20; armor/shields give half AC and double armor check penalty.

Confused: Roll d100 each round: act normally, babble, damage self, or attack nearest creature.

Dazed: Cannot act; does not lose Dex to AC.

Entangled: If anchored, cannot move; otherwise half speed. -2 attacks, -4 Dex, concentration checks to cast.

Exhausted: Half speed, -6 Str/Dex. Rest 1 hour becomes fatigued.

Fatigued: Cannot run or charge; -2 Str/Dex. Rest 8 hours to recover.

Frightened: As shaken, and must flee if possible.

Grappled: Cannot move; -4 Dex, -2 attacks/CMB except to grapple or escape; cannot make AoOs.

Helpless: Dex 0, melee attacks get +4; vulnerable to coup de grace and sneak attack.

Incorporeal: Immune to nonmagical attacks; half damage from magic, full from force/incorporeal effects.

Invisible: +2 attacks; target loses Dex to AC; +20 Stealth moving or +40 standing still.

Nauseated: Can only take move actions; no attacking, casting, or concentrating.

Panicked: As frightened, drops held items and flees all danger.

Paralyzed: Str/Dex 0, helpless; winged fliers fall.

Pinned: As grappled, also flat-footed, -4 AC, limited to verbal/mental actions except escape.

Prone: -4 melee attacks; cannot use most ranged weapons; +4 AC vs ranged, -4 AC vs melee.

Shaken: -2 attacks, weapon damage, saves, skills, and ability checks.

Sickened: -2 attacks, weapon damage, saves, skills, and ability checks.

Staggered: One move or standard action per turn, plus free/swift/immediate actions.

Stunned: Cannot act, drops held items, -2 AC, loses Dex to AC.
